negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Capability::try_from(6) does not return Capability::Fhe and Capability::try_from(7) is not invalid; the audited code maps Fhe to value 10.
related_but_insufficient_code: Capability::try_from maps 6 to Dkms, 7 to ImageCall, and 10 to Fhe; tests assert the same mapping.
Capability::Fhe is included in is_executor_capability, but not at the numeric value required by this invariant.

notes: The executor-capability classification is present, but the required numeric ABI mapping is not.
rationale: The primary diff adds Capability::Fhe = 6, maps byte value 6 to that variant, includes it in is_executor_capability, and updates tests so 6 succeeds and 7 is the first invalid value. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Capability::try_from(6) must return Capability::Fhe, Capability::try_from(7) must remain invalid, and Capability::Fhe must be classified as an executor capability.
suggested_falsification_tests: Parse registry entries containing capability bytes 5, 6, and 7; assert 5 maps to ZkCall, 6 maps to Fhe and is_executor_capability returns true, and 7 returns Err.
summary: FHE capability numbering does not match the invariant.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Assert Capability::try_from(6) == Capability::Fhe and Capability::try_from(7).is_err(); current code returns Dkms and ImageCall respectively.
title: FHE capability value is recognized as executor service
